title: Aluminum rose to 3,575.75 USD/T on May 5, 2026
summary: Aluminum prices rose 1.63% to $3,575.75 per ton on May 5, 2026. UK aluminum futures rebounded to $3,530 per tonne from a three-week low of $3,480 on April 29th, driven by expectations of prolonged supply disruptions from the Middle East.

title: Aluminum Price Analysis Today - April 27, 2026
summary: Global aluminum edged up 0.10% to $3,596.04 per ton, with Chinese benchmarks at ¥24,535 per ton. The marginal movement reflects tension between critically low LME inventories and the Strait of Hormuz blockade threatening 9% of global primary supply, though early signs of restored shipping and easing geopolitical tensions have capped further rallies.

title: Aluminum Price at 3,599.85 - Monthly Trends
summary: Aluminum prices are currently at 3,599.85, up 6.73% from last month (3,372.95) and up 35.42% compared to one year ago (2,658.29). This reflects significant year-over-year growth in aluminum valuations.

title: US to build largest aluminum plant with 500,000-ton annual capacity
summary: The United States is preparing to establish its first aluminum smelter in over three decades with an annual production capacity of 500,000 tons. Century Aluminum will undertake this initiative in Inola, Oklahoma, with a $500 million grant from the Clean Demonstrations program and partnership with Emirates Global Aluminum. The facility will produce high-purity aluminum designated for defense uses, marking a significant shift in the country's aluminum production strategy.

title: America's Aluminum Supply Hits Zero in 30 Days
summary: The United States is facing a serious industrial supply crisis with Bloomberg warning that American aluminum supplies could hit zero within 30 days. A 50% tariff on Canadian aluminum designed to protect domestic production has triggered a chain reaction threatening the entire American manufacturing base. Production disruptions across the Middle East have left American manufacturers with almost no major foreign sources to fall back on.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: Century Aluminum has decided to boost production to full capacity amid President Donald Trump's tariffs on aluminum. This move reflects the company's response to current market conditions and supply pressures.

title: Supply crunch drives aluminum to four year high
summary: Stephen Hare, lead economist at Oxford Economics, discusses the outlook and current state of aluminum amid supply constraints. The supply crunch has driven aluminum prices to a four-year high.

title: Goodbye to critical metals? Scientists create a new revolutionary aluminum
summary: Scientists have developed a new aluminum with revolutionary properties that could redefine production processes and significantly reduce dependence on critical metals, which are currently expensive and limited. This innovation could help slash industrial costs and accelerate the energy transition.

title: Ball Corporation reports record 2025 results as aluminium sustainability metrics advance
summary: Ball Corporation published its 2025 Combined Annual and Sustainability Report, highlighting record-breaking financial results and significant progress on sustainability targets. The company achieved 74% recycled aluminum usage across its global beverage packaging business in 2025.

title: Green Aluminum: How Emerus is reducing its CO₂ footprint and building a sustainable future
summary: Emerus focuses on reducing its CO₂ footprint through green aluminum production methods as part of building a sustainable future. The company emphasizes that sustainability is not a trend but a market necessity.

title: Steel Prices Rise to 3219 CNY/T, Supported by Strong Seasonal Demand
summary: Steel rebar futures rose toward CNY 3,200 per ton, reaching their highest levels since August 2025, driven by strong seasonal demand and restocking from end users. Chinese steelmakers have raised domestic delivery prices reflecting higher input costs linked to the Middle East conflict. The World Steel Association projects global steel demand will increase 0.3% this year to 1.724 billion tons.

title: HRC Steel Falls to 1132.96 USD/T Amid Market Volatility
summary: HRC Steel declined 0.44% to 1,132.96 USD/T on May 6, 2026. Despite the daily decline, HRC Steel prices have risen 5.00% over the past month and are up 27.30% year-over-year.

title: US Manufacturing Growth Driven by Steel and Aluminum Price Increases
summary: US manufacturing activity grew in April for the fourth consecutive month, with the ISM purchasing managers index at 52.7. The prices index surged to 84.6, the highest since April 2022, driven by increases in steel and aluminum prices, tariffs, and petroleum-based products due to Middle East conflict.

title: Sheet and Plate Prices Remain on Upward Trend
summary: Sheet and plate prices continued their upward trajectory as industry sources increasingly questioned whether improved demand might be driving the market as much as limited supply. US steel shipments increased in March compared to both February and year-over-year levels.

title: US Steel Production Reaches 80.4% Capacity Utilization
summary: Domestic raw steel production reached 1,856,000 net tons in the week ending May 2, 2026, with a capability utilization rate of 80.4 percent. This represents the highest capacity utilization level since August 2024.

title: US Manufacturing Activity Grows for Fourth Consecutive Month
summary: US manufacturing activity continued to expand in April for the fourth consecutive month, with order growth outpacing production. Steel prices were boosted by Middle East Gulf tensions.

title: US Steel Production Increases in March
summary: Steel production in the United States increased to 7,200 thousand tonnes in March 2026, up from 6,500 thousand tonnes in February. This reflects continued growth in domestic steel manufacturing.

title: SUMMSEED Project: Creating More Sustainable Steel with Lower Emissions
summary: SUMMSEED is an international project focused on developing more sustainable steel with a lower greenhouse gas emissions footprint compared to steel manufactured using traditional techniques. The project represents innovation in reducing the environmental impact of steel production.

title: European Commission Unveils Action Plan to Safeguard EU Steel Production
summary: The European Commission has announced a comprehensive action plan designed to protect EU steel production through import restrictions, enhancements to the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), and support for low-carbon steel technologies. This initiative aims to strengthen the competitiveness of European steelmakers while promoting sustainable production methods.

title: US Energy Department Pilot Project Measures Greenhouse Gas Intensity of Steel
summary: The US Energy Department launched a pilot project to measure the greenhouse gas intensity of industrial products, including steel. This measurement system aims to help domestic steelmakers differentiate themselves from competitors and support efforts to tackle steel emissions.

title: World Steel Association Recognizes 14 Companies as Steel Sustainability Champions
summary: The World Steel Association (worldsteel) has recognized 14 companies as Steel Sustainability Champions for their work in 2025. This recognition highlights industry leaders advancing sustainable practices in steel production.

title: Copper futures rose above $6 per pound on Wednesday
summary: Copper futures advanced for a second straight session, rising above $6 per pound amid improving sentiment around a potential US-Iran agreement. The rally is supported by signs of improving demand in China and stronger-than-expected manufacturing expansion, despite ongoing headwinds from Middle East conflict.

title: Chilean miner Codelco's El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco announced that its El Teniente smelter is set to restart on Thursday following a collapse. This development is relevant to copper supply dynamics in the current market.

title: Copper prices hit a new record high
summary: Copper has hit a fresh record high, with Bloomberg Intelligence and Blue Line Futures analysts weighing in on whether the metals trade is 'stretched'. The analysis includes expert commentary on commodity market movements.

title: London copper prices steady after hitting three-week low
summary: London copper prices stabilized after falling to a three-week low on Tuesday. The article references recent market volatility in copper trading.

title: US imposes sanctions on Congo armed group, mining firms over illicit minerals
summary: The United States announced sanctions against an armed group and mining firms in Congo related to illicit mineral extraction. This impacts copper production in the Democratic Republic of Congo, a major copper producer.

title: Copper price analysis: Chinese demand offsets stalled US-Iran peace talks
summary: Analysis of copper price movements driven by Chinese demand factors and geopolitical developments. The article examines how international relations impact copper market dynamics.

title: Innovations to close the copper supply-demand gap
summary: Explores how new technologies and favorable policies can bridge the looming copper supply-demand gap as demand grows from 26 million tonnes in 2024 to 35 million tonnes by 2035. Highlights novel grinding technologies and innovations to exploit primary sulfide ores, improve mining efficiency, and reduce environmental impacts like water usage and habitat destruction.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: Discusses copper's critical role in enabling a net-zero future through its ability to efficiently transmit electricity and minimize energy losses. Emphasizes copper's importance as the world transitions to sustainable energy infrastructure.

title: Technology Minerals unlocks new innovation and revenue stream with copper and aluminium recovery
summary: Highlights Technology Minerals' innovations in copper and aluminium recovery from batteries, creating new revenue streams and sustainable solutions. Focuses on circular economy approaches to copper supply challenges.

title: Nickel rose to 19913.88 USD/T on May 6, 2026, up 1.42% from the previous day
summary: Nickel futures traded above $19,400, marking a near two-year high as supply disruptions and tightening raw material availability reinforced bullish momentum. Prices were underpinned by mining quota cuts in Indonesia and a sulfur shortage linked to disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, which raised processing costs for nickel intermediates.

title: Nickel Futures Hover Near Multi-Month Highs
summary: Nickel prices continued their upward trajectory with a 1.42% gain on May 6, 2026. Over the past month, nickel's price has risen 16.97%, driven by supply constraints from Indonesian mining quota cuts and additional pressure from sulfur shortages affecting downstream processing.

title: The current price of Nickel futures is 19,927.25
summary: Nickel futures reached 19,927.25 USD with a previous close of 19,607.00, reflecting continued strength in the market. Despite UBS warnings of persistent surplus through 2026, recent production cuts and supply disruptions continue to support prices.

title: Indonesia nickel makers trim battery-feed output as sulphur squeeze bites
summary: Indonesian nickel processors have trimmed output by at least 10% due to a sulphur shortage caused by disruptions from the Iran war. The cuts affect plants processing nickel ore into mixed hydroxide precipitate (MHP) for EV battery materials, with facilities backed by Chinese firms like Huayou Cobalt, Lygend Resources, and Tsingshan Group impacted. While overall nickel output remains high, MHP supply is tightening with low inventories at several firms.

title: Nickel rose to 19942.50 USD/T on May 6, 2026
summary: Nickel futures traded above $19,400, marking a near two-year high driven by supply disruptions and tightening raw material availability. Mining quota cuts in Indonesia have constrained ore supply, while a sulfur shortage linked to Strait of Hormuz disruptions has raised processing costs and tightened output conditions for downstream processors.

title: Nickel Headed to $20,000 as Indonesia Limits Production, EV Demand Surges
summary: Analysts expect nickel prices to reach $20,000 per ton within the next month as Indonesia commits to flat-to-down ore production targets of 250-260 million tons for 2026. EV demand remains robust with 20% underlying growth, while nickel consumption is projected to grow 6-7% annually, supported by completed destocking in the lithium supply chain.

title: Green Memory: How Nickel Could Unlock the Future of Sustainable AI
summary: The NICKEFFECT project, led by Singulus Technologies in collaboration with the Universitat Autònoma de Barcelona and AGH University Krakow, has developed nickel‑based MRAM stacks that replace critical platinum with abundant nickel. These new stacks remain stable up to 410°C, exceeding the 370°C standard for industrial chip manufacturing, and are positioned as a low‑power, green storage technology for sustainable AI.

title: Nickel Industry News, Views and Commentary - Chronickels
summary: The Nickel Institute’s blog series ‘Chronickels’ covers sustainability, treatment processes, and innovations in nickel production, starting with nickel ores. The articles highlight new approaches to making nickel production more environmentally friendly and resource‑efficient.

title: Nickel | Innovation News Network
summary: This section aggregates the latest nickel supply chain news and analysis, focusing on innovative ways to produce and use nickel as an energy metal. It emphasizes sustainable production methods and new applications of nickel in clean energy technologies.

title: The latest nickel mining news
summary: Global Mining Review’s nickel‑mining tag page tracks recent developments in nickel mining, including technology‑driven efficiency improvements and sustainability initiatives such as the Nickel Mark sustainability award. summary: The Nickel Institute’s blog provides ongoing commentary on nickel industry trends, including technological innovations and sustainability efforts across the value chain. It serves as a resource for updates on how the sector is adapting to environmental and regulatory pressures.

summary: Nornickel’s news and press‑release section includes updates on the company’s operations, ESG initiatives, and technological innovations aimed at improving sustainability in nickel production. summary: Genomines’ blog features a recent T&E paper examining the global nickel market, Europe’s potential role, and pathways to sourcing nickel sustainably. The analysis underscores how technological and policy innovations can support a more sustainable nickel supply chain.

summary: Tenaris has been recognized as a worldsteel Sustainability Champion for the ninth consecutive time, demonstrating its ongoing commitment to sustainable practices in steel production.
